data.title: HLTH, ESIH, HLT: Give Your Portfolio a Lift with European Healthcare ETFs
data.description:
Investors worried about volatility in the U.S. stock market should look to Europe and several advantageous healthcare exchange-traded funds.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The American market remains volatile as investors worry about the impacts of artificial intelligence (AI), as well as the direction of interest rates and health of the economy. Market gyrations have grown more extreme since the start of 2026.

Many analysts are recommending that investors look to Europe for diversification and gains. European stocks have outperformed U.S. equities since Donald Trump returned to the White House. And Europe is home to leading healthcare companies such as Novo Nordisk (NVO), AstraZeneca (AZN), and Sanofi (SNY).

European Healthcare ETFs

Leading European healthcare ETFs include the SPDR MSCI Europe Health Care ETF (HLTH), which tracks the performance of the MSCI Europe Health Care Index, providing targeted exposure to large and mid-cap healthcare companies across developed European markets.

There's also the iShares MSCI Europe Health Care Sector ETF (ESIH), the largest and most liquid ETF that tracks the MSCI Europe Health Care index. And the Amundi STOXX Europe 600 Healthcare ETF (AU:HLTH), which follows the broader STOXX Europe 600 Healthcare index and includes 55 leading healthcare firms. Below is a chart comparing these ETFs.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Forecast Ahead of Earnings – Here's What to Expect
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results after markets close on February 26. The cloud infrastructure provider has seen sharp volatility amid recent AI fears, with shares down 4.4% over the past month. CoreWeave specializes in high-performance Nvidia (NVDA) GPUs (graphics processing units) for training large AI models and serves large clients like OpenAI and Meta (META) across 33+ data centers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Execution risk remains the prime concern for the neo-cloud provider. Investors will scrutinize management comments for signs of further delays in AI infrastructure buildouts. In Q3, summer weather halted progress at its Denton, Texas data center, forcing cuts to 2025 capex and revenue forecasts.

Expectations from CoreWeave

The Street expects CoreWeave to post an adjusted loss of $0.50 per share, significantly wider than $0.08 per share reported last year. Sales are forecast to surge about 105% year-over-year to $1.53 billion. However, compared to Q3FY25, the figure shows a modest rise of 12.5%.

Meanwhile, the company has guided for full-year 2025 sales of $5.05-5.15 billion and adjusted EBIT (earnings before interest and taxes) between $690 and $720 million.

Analysts' Views Ahead of Results

Citizens JMP analyst Gregory Miller reiterated his Buy rating and $180 price target, implying 97.8% upside potential. He stated that CoreWeave leads in renting out powerful GPUs as a service (GPUaaS) and is well-placed to meet booming AI infrastructure needs, with multi-year deals and over $55.6 billion in revenue backlog. The market for GPU rentals is exploding due to faster Gen AI adoption and big cloud companies outsourcing more.

Risks like falling prices, customer concentration, and high debt persist. Still, CoreWeave's top market position, clear future contracts, and growth potential make CRWV a strong short- to medium-term AI infrastructure play.

Similarly, D.A. Davidson analyst Alex Platt kept his Buy rating and $110 price target, suggesting 20.9% upside, after Nvidia infused another $2 billion in CoreWeave. Nvidia is helping CoreWeave reduce risks in building 5 GW of data centers by 2030, supporting massive compute demand and sustainable growth.

Is CRWV Stock a Good Buy?

On TipRanks, CoreWeave has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and eight Hold ratings. The average CoreWeave price target of $118.57 implies 30.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, CRWV shares have skyrocketed over 145%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: CVNA Earnings: Carvana's Profit Blows Past Estimates as Used-Vehicle Demand Soars
data.description:
Carvana (CVNA) has reported a fourth-quarter 2025 profit that blew past Wall Street forecasts on surging demand for the company's used vehicles.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The online used car retailer announced earnings per share (EPS) $4.22, which was far ahead of the $1.14 consensus estimate among analysts. Revenue in the final three months of last year totaled $5.60 billion, which topped the $5.27 billion forecast on Wall Street.

Management attributed the blowout print to to strong demand for used vehicles among American consumers who are struggling with inflation, a higher cost of living, and the impacts of tariffs. Based in Arizona, Carvana is known for its car vending machines that house its motor vehicles.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rally as Fed Eyes Rate Hikes to Counter Inflation; Industrial Sector Posts Solid Growth
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Wednesday in positive territory, although a warning from the Fed put investors on high alert.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead of rate cuts, higher interest rates could be on the table if inflation remains above 2%, minutes from January's Fed meeting showed. Inflation remains a key concern for many officials, with several considering the "possibility that upward adjustments to the target range for the federal funds rate could be appropriate if inflation remains at above-target levels."

The minutes also noted that a majority of officials believe the risk of persistent inflation is still present, while downside risks to employment have softened in recent months. Lower rates can benefit the labor market but risk pushing inflation higher through increased borrowing and consumer spending.

The Fed will announce its next interest rate decision on March 18, with 94.1% odds of rates staying unchanged on the CME FedWatch tool. By year-end, the most likely scenario is for the central bank to cut rates by 50 bps to 3.00%-3.25%.

Meanwhile, the Trump administration's goal of increasing domestic production is gaining ground. In January, U.S. industrial production rose by 0.7% from the prior month, marking the highest rate of growth since February 2025. Several factors may have contributed to the growth, including easing trade uncertainty and favorable tax policies. Efficiency is also on the rise, with factory capacity utilization reaching 75.6% during the month, the highest since September and improving from 75.2% in December.

Elsewhere, the S&P 500 (SPX) is having its worst start to the year compared to global peers since 1995. The benchmark index is essentially flat year-to-date, while the iShares MSCI ACWI ex U.S. ETF (ACWX), which tracks global markets excluding the U.S., has returned 7.4%. Geopolitical tensions and volatile economic policies, including tariffs, President Trump's threat to annex Greenland, and concerns about overspending in the AI industry, have pushed many investors to shift their capital away from the U.S.

U.S. tensions with Iran have also unsettled some investors. While both sides touted progress in nuclear talks on Tuesday, Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei continues to send out aggressive statements on X, including a post that warned the U.S. military "may at times be struck so hard that it cannot get up again."

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.56% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.80%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) Dethrones Polsat to Retake Top Distributor Slot
data.description:
While much of what is going on around entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) is focused on the upcoming buyout—though just who will do the buying is unclear as yet—Warner still functions as its own business operation. And now, it just achieved a major milestone in Poland, taking back the top slot in content distributor rankings in the country. Investors were oddly displeased, and sent shares down fractionally in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The word from the All Screens Video Landscape Report from Nielsen revealed that Warner actually managed to budge Polsat out of the top slot. This was also the only major change in the distributor rankings for January 2026. This is actually something of a coup for Warner, as it had not held the top slot in the rankings since May 2025.

Warner managed to take 18.35% of the market, up from 17.59% in December 2025. That was enough to beat Polsat, which saw its January 2026 numbers drop to 17.54%. TVP landed the third slot at 17.21%. Interestingly, Poles in general watched more television in January. This is not a surprise given the nature of a Polish winter, and this winter was considered "…exceptionally harsh…" by some reports.

"Deliberate Design Choice"

Meanwhile, Warner joined a range of other media companies in throwing cease-and-desist orders at ByteDance, TikTok's owner, over the release of its Seedance AI system. Warner demanded that ByteDance stop using Warner content to train its AI, and actively block users from sharing videos that featured copyrighted characters.

But the response may actually be too late. A recently-seen video of Tom Cruise fighting Brad Pitt, which reports described as "stunningly realistic," left some with deep concerns about the future of filmmaking as a whole. Rhett Reese, who wrote Zombieland and Deadpool & Wolverine, noted "I hate to say it…it's likely over for us."

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on four Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 164% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $29.13 per share implies 1.22% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: "Powerful AI Alliance": Stifel Analyst Renews Bullish Call on Nvidia Stock (NVDA) after Expanded Meta Deal
data.description:
Stifel analyst Ruben Roy reaffirmed his Buy rating and $250 price target on Nvidia (NVDA) stock, citing the company's newly expanded partnership with Meta Platforms (META) as a positive driver.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Yesterday, Nvidia announced a multi-year, multi-generational collaboration with Meta to build out on-premises, cloud, and AI infrastructure. The deal expands the companies' existing relationship and includes large-scale deployments of Nvidia's CPUs, GPUs, and Spectrum‑X Ethernet networking technology.

A key highlight is Meta's plan to use Nvidia's rack-scale Confidential Computing for WhatsApp private processing. Roy noted that this adoption reflects Meta's confidence in Nvidia's full-stack approach to AI systems.

Analyst's Key Takeaways From the Deal

Roy pointed to several important elements of the expanded partnership. He said this deal marks the first major rollout of Nvidia's Grace CPU-only systems. Nvidia and Meta will support the deployment with joint investments in infrastructure co-design and software optimization, a sign of how closely the two companies are now working together.

Further, Meta's commitment adds more momentum to Nvidia's Spectrum‑X Ethernet networking platform, which is built specifically for AI workloads. Roy sees this as further proof that Nvidia's networking strategy is gaining traction.

Lastly, Roy views Meta's use of Confidential Computing technology as a strong sign of confidence in Nvidia's security-focused AI architecture.

While hyperscalers will keep developing their own custom silicon and networking solutions, Roy expects Nvidia to remain highly competitive due to its full-stack architecture, spanning chips, systems, networking, and software.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 37 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $260.06 indicates 38.57% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Elliott Wants a Norwegian Cruise Line Comeback. Citi Says It Won't Be That Easy
data.description:
Activist investment firm Elliott Management recently revealed that it has built a 10% stake in Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), which means that it is likely to push for changes at the cruise line. In a letter to shareholders, Elliott argued that years of weak performance are the result of a board that lacks relevant expertise, a CEO without deep cruise industry experience, and cost structures that are out of line with competitors. As a result, Elliott is calling for a major reset to restore profitable growth and is targeting more than $4 billion in EBITDA by 2027, well above current Wall Street estimates of $3.35 billion.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, not everyone believes that goal is realistic. In fact, five-star Citi (C) analyst James Hardiman warned that reaching $4 billion in EBITDA by 2027 would require a dramatic turnaround, which he sees as highly challenging. To begin with, Norwegian must compete directly with Royal Caribbean (RCL), a stronger and better-capitalized rival that is targeting many of the same customers. While he stated that this competitive pressure does not excuse Norwegian's struggles, it does highlight how difficult it will be to regain lost ground quickly.

In addition, although Norwegian's cost trends have improved in recent years, Hardiman noted that those reductions come after costs ballooned under former CEO Frank Del Rio during what he described as a "cost is no object" period. Moreover, plans to invest in private destinations may not fully close the gap either, as Norwegian still trails competitors in that area. Still, Hardiman believes that with steadier leadership, better execution, and modest margin gains, Norwegian could potentially reach the $4 billion EBITDA mark by 2028, which might justify a higher valuation multiple.

Is NCLH Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NCLH stock based on six Buys, six Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NCLH price target of $26.82 per share implies 10.2% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slips on New Concerns Over Nvidia/Meta Partnership
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) may have a new problem to watch out for going forward, as one of its biggest supporters, Nvidia (NVDA) seems to be hedging its bets. Nvidia recently stepped up its partnership with Meta Platforms (META), and that combination could pose problems for Intel going forward. Investors seemed concerned as well, and Intel shares were down nearly 1.5% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia plans to put more of its Grace central processing units (CPUs) into place at Meta data centers. Meta is about to launch what reports call "…the first large-scale Nvidia Grace-only deployment." That alone might not mean so much, but some are concerned that this is a sign that Intel may be about to face a lot harder competition in the data center space, a point that Intel was clearly leading previously.

Worse for Intel, Nvidia has a better competitive proposition than merely "at least we're not Intel." Nvidia reports that its chips offer better performance per watt for the data center. Given the increasing difficulty of finding watts to run data centers these days, more power-efficient chips are worth considering. In Meta's case, they were worth going with over Intel.

Many Happy Returns

This brings us to the issue of a recent Turkish wedding. I know, that is one seriously strange segue, but stick with me. A normal Turkish wedding often involves high-end gifts for the bride and groom, normally, gold and jewelry. But in this particular wedding, the bride and groom landed an array of computer hardware instead.

The groom, at one point, was caught sporting an MSI Suprim GeForce RTX 5090 GPU, which was hung around his neck with a length of red ribbon. The bride picked up a quad-channel DDR5 memory kit, again adorned in red ribbon. Apparently, the value of these things in the Turkish market is actually exceeding gold, so in keeping with Turkish wedding tradition, the bride and groom landed the best of computer hardware.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 79.55% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 6.05% upside potential.

And lets put the image here.

data.title: Fed Warns of Rate Hikes if Inflation Remains Above 2%, Minutes Show
data.description:
The minutes from January's Fed meeting showed that some Fed officials are considering hiking interest rates in order to tame inflation, which remains above the central bank's target of 2%. During the meeting, the Fed voted 10-2 to leave rates unchanged.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Several participants considered the "possibility that upward adjustments to the target range for the federal funds rate could be appropriate if inflation remains at above-target levels."

Fed Signals Patience on Rate Policy

The majority of officials agreed that persistent inflation is still a key risk, while downside risks to employment have eased in recent months. Lower interest rates can help support the labor market, although they can also increase inflation by encouraging spending and borrowing.

Some officials worried that cutting rates while inflation remains elevated could be viewed as a weakening commitment to the 2% inflation target, possibly making higher prices "more entrenched."

The next interest rate meeting is scheduled for March 18, where the Fed is widely expected to hold rates steady.
